 Pyrethrum, also known as Pyrethrum germanica, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, treating fever, and alleviating pain. The bioactive constituents of Pyrethrum include pyrethrins, which are a group of compounds including pyrethrin I and II, cinerolone, and jasmolin, that have insecticidal and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations made from Pyrethrum include tinctures, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and insect bites.
